Defines built-in function properties and identifiers for the ECMA script engine.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines macros and uses them to describe built-in properties of a JavaScript `Function` object within an ECMAScript engine. The file uses conditional preprocessor directives to define macros such as `OBJECT_ID`, `OBJECT_VALUE`, `NUMBER_VALUE`, and `ROUTINE` if they are not already defined. These macros are then used to specify the identifier and properties of the `Function` object, including its prototype and length. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `Function` object, while `OBJECT_VALUE` and `NUMBER_VALUE` define the `prototype` and `length` properties, respectively, with fixed attributes. The file concludes by undefining the macros to prevent potential conflicts with other code.
